rag_rules: Repair only AGREE clauses with unresolved references by aligning identifiers to visible AADL features or deleting invalid clauses; do not invent new declarations.
When diagnostics reference implementation-level subcomponents or ports in a type annex, move bindings to implementation scope or remove them.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or locally declared eq/const helpers in AGREE expressions.
Preserve type consistency: Boolean expressions must evaluate to bool, numeric to int/real, and never assume AADL properties are automatically accessible as variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE uses 'int', 'real', 'bool' as scalar types—never 'integer' or 'boolean'.
Assign statements are valid only in implementation annexes and must target existing outputs or auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not resolve unresolved identifiers by inventing new ports, types, or package imports.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
